rulings.Case Title: ELAINE PERCY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 25, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that all of plaintiffs' claims against it fail even if defendant owed no duty to Elaine Percy. Duty is not an element of strict products liability. (Elsheref v. Applied Materials, Inc. (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 451.) Defendant moved solely for summary judgment and did not include an alternate request for summary adjudication of individual causes of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS LEO DOUGLAS TREMBLAY VS. ADERHOLT SPECIALTY COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To Deft San Francisco Gravel Co., Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 25, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiffs' motion for summary adjudication as to defendant San Francisco Gravel Co., Inc. is continued on the court's own motion to October 9, 2014. This continuance is due to the Court's availability, not defendant's C.C.P. section 437c(h) request. Additionally, plaintiffs must deliver to the Court courtesy copies of (1) the declaration of Laurel Halbany filed with the moving papers, including all attachments, and (2) plaintiffs' reply papers, neither of which the Court ever received. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: TRUJILLO, MARY ANN, AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO et al VS. DURO DYNE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cahill Construction, Co., Inc., Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint For Survival, Wrongful Death
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 25, 2014 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc.'s motion to strike portions of Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint for survival and wrongful death is denied. The Court will grant defendant's request for judicial notice, Exhibits A and B. Nevertheless the motion is denied because Plaintiffs met the requirements under C.C.P. § 3294. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: WAYNE HANSEN VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Fdcc California, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 25, 2014 in Department 503, Line 6. Defendant FDCC California, Inc.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiff's complaint is denied. The complaint alleges sufficient facts to support a claim for punitive damages and defendant did not demonstrate that such a claim is barred against a dissolved corporation. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
